chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment of Slaves in Adultery

text: 5054 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ نُعَيْمِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ مِسْمَعٍ أَبِي سَيَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "أُمُّ اَلْوَلَدِ جِنَايَتُهَا فِي حُقُوقِ اَلنَّاسِ عَلَى سَيِّدِهَا" قَالَ "وَ مَا كَانَ مِنْ حَقِّ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي اَلْحُدُودِ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ فِي بَدَنِهَا" وَ قَالَ "يُقَاصُّ مِنْهَا لِلْمَمَالِيكِ وَ لاَ قِصَاصَ بَيْنَ اَلْحُرِّ وَ اَلْعَبْدِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5054 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Nuaym ibn Ibrahim from Mismaʿ Abu Sayyar from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "The liability of umm Al-walad (a slave woman who has borne a child to her master) concerning the rights of people is upon her master."
Imam (as) also said: "As for the rights of Allah (swt), the Exalted, regarding prescribed punishments, that is upon her own body."
Furthermore, Imam (as) said: "Retribution (qiṣaṣ) applies between her and other slaves, but there is no retribution between a free person and a slave."
